_nad_metabolism_update.mechanism: NOTE: Clinical endpoint evidence for NMN/NR is null in latest large RCTs despite increases in blood NAD+ levels

outcomes.description: NAD+ precursor supplementation raises blood NAD+ but does not improve clinical outcomes in current evidence

actions.title: Reconsider NMN/NR supplementation
actions.description: Latest large RCT (NR-long COVID-2025) confirms no clinical endpoint benefit from NAD+ precursors alone

_telomere_update.mechanism: NOTE: The 2022 PNAS MCMV-TERT paper claiming +41% mouse lifespan was retracted August 2025

outcomes.description: No credible evidence that telomerase pills extend human lifespan

actions.title: Avoid telomerase supplement products
actions.description: Retracted foundational paper; no replacement evidence for pill-based telomere extension

outcomes.description: Reduced drug and toxin clearance
No safe lower threshold for stroke

actions.title: Max ~100g/week (~7 standard drinks)
actions.description: Lowest all-cause mortality threshold

outcomes.description: Modified blood vessel growth capacity

anti_il11.mechanism: IL-11 inhibition reduces pro-fibrotic and pro-inflammatory signaling in aging

outcomes.description: Age-related tissue fibrosis driven in part by IL-11 signaling
IL-11 contributes to multi-organ age-related decline

actions.title: Monitor anti-IL-11 clinical trials
actions.description: Anti-IL-11 antibody extended mouse lifespan 25% late in life; already in human trials for fibrotic lung disease

outcomes.description: Reduced ability to resolve inflammation

aortopathy_risk.mechanism: Genetic variants affecting connective tissue and vascular integrity, primarily in FBN1 (Marfan syndrome), TGFBR1/TGFBR2 (Loeys-Dietz syndrome), COL3A1 (vascular Ehlers-Danlos), ACTA2, and MYH11, leading to aortic root dilation, aneurysm, and dissection

outcomes.description: Progressive aortic root or ascending aortic aneurysm formation
Life-threatening aortic dissection risk, particularly at younger ages
Skeletal, ocular, and cardiovascular manifestations in Marfan syndrome
Arterial tortuosity and aneurysm risk in Loeys-Dietz syndrome

actions.title: Baseline aortic imaging
actions.description: Echocardiogram and CT/MRI for aortic root and entire aorta measurement

actions.title: Serial aortic surveillance
actions.description: Annual imaging to monitor aortic growth rate and determine surgical timing

actions.title: Beta-blocker or ARB therapy
actions.description: Reduce aortic wall stress with atenolol or losartan to slow dilation

actions.title: Elective aortic root replacement
actions.description: Prophylactic valve-sparing root replacement at threshold diameters

actions.title: Pregnancy counseling and monitoring
actions.description: High-risk pregnancy monitoring; discuss dissection risk and surgical timing before conception

actions.title: Activity restriction
actions.description: Avoid heavy lifting, contact sports, and isometric exercise that increases aortic wall stress

apoB_management.mechanism: Apolipoprotein B as direct measure of atherogenic particle count

outcomes.description: Elevated apoB drives atherosclerosis independently of LDL-C

actions.title: Request apoB test (more predictive than LDL-C)
actions.description: Target: <80 mg/dL primary prevention, <60 mg/dL high-risk

actions.title: Statin therapy if apoB elevated
actions.description: Each 1 mmol/L LDL-C reduction = ~21% lower vascular events/year (CTT meta-analysis)

arrhythmia.mechanism: Cardiac ion channel genes (SCN5A, KCNH2, KCNQ1) regulating electrical conduction and heart rhythm

outcomes.description: Favorable cardiac rhythm — low risk of adverse outcomes
Moderate cardiac rhythm — some genetic predisposition
Elevated cardiac rhythm — increased genetic risk requiring monitoring

actions.title: Discuss cardiac rhythm with healthcare provider
actions.description: Review findings and determine appropriate screening or monitoring

actions.title: Evidence-based heart rhythm optimization
actions.description: Implement recommended lifestyle and preventive measures

arrhythmia_risk.mechanism: Genetic variants in cardiac ion channel genes (KCNQ1, KCNH2, SCN5A, RYR2, CACNA1C) and associated proteins that alter cardiac electrophysiology and predispose to long QT syndrome, Brugada syndrome, catecholaminergic polymorphic ventricular tachycardia, and other channelopathies

outcomes.description: Long QT syndrome risk with potential for torsades de pointes and sudden cardiac death
Brugada syndrome risk with characteristic ECG pattern and ventricular arrhythmia risk
Catecholaminergic polymorphic VT risk during exercise or emotional stress
Elevated risk of sudden cardiac death if untreated

actions.title: Comprehensive ECG evaluation
actions.description: 12-lead ECG, exercise stress test, and Holter monitoring for arrhythmia screening

actions.title: Beta-blocker therapy
actions.description: Nonselective beta-blockers (nadolol, propranolol) for LQTS and CPVT

actions.title: ICD placement evaluation
actions.description: Implantable cardioverter-defibrillator for high-risk phenotypes or cardiac arrest survivors

actions.title: Avoid QTc-prolonging medications
actions.description: Avoid drugs that prolong QT interval (www.crediblemeds.org) and electrolyte abnormalities

actions.title: Lifestyle modifications for channelopathy
actions.description: Avoid intense exercise (especially swimming in LQTS), maintain potassium/magnesium levels

outcomes.description: Anemia, fatigue, neurological issues
Reduced methylation capacity

actions.title: Take methylcobalamin
actions.description: 1000mcg methylcobalamin sublingual

baseline_labs.mechanism: Comprehensive blood biomarker monitoring across seven domains: metabolic, cardiovascular, hormonal, inflammation, nutrient, liver/kidney, and cellular aging. Annual baseline recommended for healthy adults; every 3-6 months when optimizing performance or managing conditions.

outcomes.description: Silent deficiencies affecting longevity
Undetected insulin resistance or prediabetes
Unmeasured ApoB and Lp(a) - stronger predictors than standard lipids
Undiagnosed thyroid or sex hormone dysfunction

actions.title: HbA1c every 3-6 months (target <5.5%)
actions.description: 3-month average blood sugar - flags prediabetes earlier than fasting glucose

actions.title: Fasting glucose annually (optimal 70-89 mg/dL)
actions.description: Lower end of normal range associated with better outcomes

actions.title: Fasting insulin annually (optimal <6 µIU/mL)
actions.description: Earliest marker of insulin resistance - rises decades before glucose

actions.title: ApoB annually (target <80 mg/dL for low risk)
actions.description: Counts atherogenic particles directly - out-predicts LDL for plaque risk

actions.title: Lp(a) once in lifetime (genetic, stable over time)
actions.description: Genetic lipoprotein variant - elevated in ~20% of population, shrugs off most lifestyle changes

actions.title: Full lipid panel annually (LDL-C, HDL-C, triglycerides)
actions.description: Standard cardiovascular risk assessment; track trends over time

actions.title: TSH annually (optimal 0.5-2.5 mIU/L)
actions.description: Thyroid function screening - controls every cell's energy pace

actions.title: Free T3, Free T4, reverse T3 if TSH abnormal
actions.description: Full thyroid panel when screening flags issues

actions.title: Testosterone (total + free) if symptomatic
actions.description: Steers libido, muscle maintenance, mood, and sleep quality

actions.title: Cortisol (AM) if sleep or stress concerns
actions.description: Chronic elevation drives belly fat, poor sleep, and immune suppression

actions.title: hs-CRP annually (target <1.0 mg/L)
actions.description: Low-grade inflammation marker - even mild elevation signals vascular risk

actions.title: 25-OH Vitamin D annually (target 40-60 ng/mL)
actions.description: Test before supplementing - affects bone density, immune function, and mood

actions.title: B12 and ferritin annually
actions.description: B12 absorption declines with age; ferritin screens iron status and hidden inflammation

actions.title: RBC magnesium if fatigue, cramps, or arrhythmia
actions.description: Serum magnesium is poorly reflective; RBC magnesium is more accurate. Depleted by stress, sweat, and certain drugs

actions.title: ALT, AST, GGT annually
actions.description: Liver enzymes flag fatty liver (ALT), oxidative stress (GGT), and medication effects

actions.title: Creatinine and eGFR annually
actions.description: Low eGFR is an early warning for chronic kidney disease

actions.title: Uric acid annually (target <6.0 mg/dL)
actions.description: Beyond gout - elevated uric acid affects metabolism and cardiovascular risk

actions.title: Homocysteine annually (target <10 µmol/L)
actions.description: Amino acid byproduct linked to heart, brain health, and biological aging rate

blood_health.mechanism: Hematopoietic and hemoglobin genes regulating red blood cell production and oxygen transport

outcomes.description: Favorable blood cell production — low risk of adverse outcomes
Moderate blood cell production — some genetic predisposition
Elevated blood cell production — increased genetic risk requiring monitoring

actions.title: Discuss blood cell production with healthcare provider
actions.description: Review findings and determine appropriate screening or monitoring

actions.title: Evidence-based blood health optimization
actions.description: Implement recommended lifestyle and preventive measures

outcomes.description: Modified blood pressure regulation
Silent driver of cardiovascular disease

actions.title: Home BP cuff monitoring (2 weeks)
actions.description: More accurate than clinic readings

body_composition.mechanism: Lean mass vs fat mass distribution

outcomes.description: Central obesity drives metabolic disease

actions.title: Track waist circumference
actions.description: Better than scale for metabolic health

actions.title: DEXA scan for baseline body composition
actions.description: One-time baseline

actions.title: Target BMI ~25 (not 22)
actions.description: Lowest all-cause mortality

outcomes.description: Higher propensity for weight gain

bone_health.mechanism: Bone matrix and mineralization genes (collagens, growth factors, mineral transporters) regulating bone density and strength

outcomes.description: Favorable bone density — low risk of adverse outcomes
Moderate bone density — some genetic predisposition
Elevated bone density — increased genetic risk requiring monitoring

actions.title: Discuss bone density with healthcare provider
actions.description: Review findings and determine appropriate screening or monitoring

actions.title: Evidence-based bone strength optimization
actions.description: Implement recommended lifestyle and preventive measures

brain_rejuvenation.mechanism: Reversal of neuroinflammation, synaptic loss, and vascular decline in aging brain

outcomes.description: Chronic brain inflammation drives cognitive decline
Brain vascular aging contributes to dementia risk

actions.title: Regular aerobic exercise (strongest brain anti-aging intervention)
actions.description: Increases BDNF, improves cerebral blood flow, reduces neuroinflammation

actions.title: Maintain cognitive and social engagement
actions.description: Learning new skills and social connection are protective against cognitive decline

actions.title: Prioritize sleep for glymphatic clearance
actions.description: Deep sleep phase clears amyloid-beta and tau from the brain

outcomes.description: Difficulty falling asleep after caffeine consumption
Increased anxiety with caffeine

actions.title: Limit caffeine intake
actions.description: Reduce daily caffeine to < 200mg

actions.title: Avoid caffeine after 12:00
actions.description: No caffeine after noon for better sleep

calcium_metabolism.mechanism: Calcium-sensing receptor and vitamin D pathway genes regulating mineral homeostasis

outcomes.description: Favorable calcium regulation — low risk of adverse outcomes
Moderate calcium regulation — some genetic predisposition
Elevated calcium regulation — increased genetic risk requiring monitoring

actions.title: Discuss calcium regulation with healthcare provider
actions.description: Review findings and determine appropriate screening or monitoring

actions.title: Evidence-based bone and mineral health optimization
actions.description: Implement recommended lifestyle and preventive measures

caloric_restriction.mechanism: Reduced caloric intake activates mTOR inhibition and metabolic reprogramming

outcomes.description: Improved insulin sensitivity and metabolic markers
Lifespan extension proportional to restriction in animal models

actions.title: Time-restricted eating (16:8 or 14:10)
actions.description: Eat within 8-10 hour window; strongest evidence for metabolic health

actions.title: Moderate caloric reduction (10-15% below maintenance)
actions.description: CALERIE trial: sustained 2-year CR improved cardiometabolic risk factors in humans

actions.title: Avoid extreme restriction (<20% CR)
actions.description: Risk of muscle loss, bone density decline, and immune suppression. Genetics > diet for longevity outcome

cancer_susceptibility.mechanism: Inherited predisposition to malignancies from germline pathogenic variants in tumor suppressor genes, oncogenes, and DNA repair genes (e.g., BRCA1/2, TP53, MLH1, APC, CDKN2A)

outcomes.description: Elevated lifetime risk of breast cancer (BRCA1/2, PALB2, CHEK2, ATM)
Elevated risk of colorectal cancer (Lynch syndrome, FAP, MUTYH-associated polyposis)
Elevated risk of ovarian cancer (BRCA1/2, RAD51C/D, BRIP1)
Elevated risk of pancreatic cancer (CDKN2A, BRCA2, PALB2, STK11)
Elevated risk of prostate cancer (HOXB13, BRCA2, ATM)
Elevated risk of malignant melanoma (CDKN2A, MC1R, MITF, TERT)

actions.title: Genetic counseling and risk assessment
actions.description: Refer to certified genetic counselor for comprehensive risk evaluation and family history review

actions.title: Enhanced cancer screening protocol
actions.description: Earlier and more frequent screenings (mammography, colonoscopy, MRI) per NCCN guidelines

actions.title: Risk-reducing surgical options
actions.description: Discuss prophylactic mastectomy or salpingo-oophorectomy for high-penetrance variants

actions.title: Chemoprevention strategies
actions.description: Tamoxifen/raloxifene for breast cancer risk; aspirin for colorectal cancer risk

actions.title: Lifestyle risk reduction
actions.description: Maintain healthy weight, limit alcohol, avoid tobacco, sun protection, HPV vaccination

cardiomyopathy.mechanism: Cardiac muscle structural genes (MYH7, MYBPC3, TTN, LMNA) affecting heart muscle contractility and integrity

outcomes.description: Favorable cardiac muscle function — low risk of adverse outcomes
Moderate cardiac muscle function — some genetic predisposition
Elevated cardiac muscle function — increased genetic risk requiring monitoring

actions.title: Discuss cardiac muscle function with healthcare provider
actions.description: Review findings and determine appropriate screening or monitoring

actions.title: Evidence-based heart health optimization
actions.description: Implement recommended lifestyle and preventive measures

cardiomyopathy_risk.mechanism: Genetic variants in sarcomere genes (MYH7, MYBPC3, TNNT2, TNNI3), desmosomal genes (PKP2, DSP, DSG2), and other structural proteins that increase susceptibility to dilated, hypertrophic, or arrhythmogenic cardiomyopathy

outcomes.description: Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy risk from sarcomere gene variants
Dilated cardiomyopathy risk from TTN, LMNA, and other structural variants
Arrhythmogenic right ventricular cardiomyopathy risk from desmosomal variants
Progressive heart failure if cardiomyopathy develops

actions.title: Refer to cardiology for echocardiogram
actions.description: Baseline echocardiogram, ECG, and cardiac MRI for family screening

actions.title: Serial cardiac monitoring
actions.description: Regular echocardiograms every 1-3 years to detect early structural changes

actions.title: Activity restriction guidance
actions.description: Avoid competitive sports and intense exertion in HCM and ARVC phenotypes

actions.title: Beta-blocker prophylaxis
actions.description: Consider beta-blockers for HCM with obstruction or high-risk features

actions.title: Cascade family screening
actions.description: First-degree relatives should undergo clinical screening and variant testing

cardiotoxicity_risk.mechanism: Genetic variants affecting drug metabolism, transport, and cardiac ion channels that increase susceptibility to chemotherapy-induced cardiomyopathy (anthracyclines, trastuzumab), antiarrhythmic drug toxicity, and other drug-induced cardiac adverse events

outcomes.description: Doxorubicin and related anthracycline-induced cardiomyopathy, potentially irreversible
Trastuzumab-related left ventricular dysfunction, typically reversible
Proarrhythmia or toxicity from antiarrhythmic medications
Tyrosine kinase inhibitor-associated hypertension and heart failure

actions.title: Baseline cardiac function assessment
actions.description: LVEF assessment by echocardiogram or MUGA before cardiotoxic therapy

actions.title: Cardioprotective regimen
actions.description: Dexrazoxane for anthracycline cardioprotection in high-risk patients

actions.title: On-treatment cardiac monitoring
actions.description: Serial troponin and LVEF monitoring during and after chemotherapy

actions.title: Consider alternative chemotherapy
actions.description: Liposomal doxorubicin formulations or non-anthracycline regimens for high-risk patients

actions.title: Early heart failure therapy
actions.description: Initiate ACE inhibitor and beta-blocker at earliest sign of LV dysfunction

outcomes.description: Impaired blood vessel function
Increased blood pressure risk
Strong predictor of all-cause mortality

actions.title: Get baseline VO2max test
actions.description: Lab test (~$200) or smartwatch estimate

actions.title: 2x/week HIIT: 4x4min hard / 3min easy
actions.description: 10-min warmup + 4 rounds + cool down (~40 min total)

actions.title: 1-2x/week Zone 2 cardio at conversational pace
actions.description: 45-60 min at ~60-70% max HR

outcomes.description: Modified cell cycle control
Very high LDL cholesterol
Modified HDL cholesterol function
Natural tendency for later sleep timing

actions.title: Get bright light exposure in morning

clinvar_pathogenic.mechanism: Identification of a genetic variant classified as Pathogenic (P) or Likely Pathogenic (LP) in ClinVar, an NIH-aggregated database of human genetic variation and phenotype relationships. These variants have established or strong evidence of association with Mendelian disease based on ACMG/AMP classification criteria

outcomes.description: Pathogenic variant in an ACMG-recommended actionable gene (e.g., BRCA1, MLH1, LDLR, RYR1, KCNQ1)
Pathogenic variant in a gene without established medical management guidelines
Pathogenic variant carrier for a recessive condition; no clinical phenotype but reproductive implications
Pathogenic variant affecting drug metabolism or response with established PGx guidance

actions.title: Referral to clinical genetics
actions.description: Comprehensive evaluation by clinical geneticist and genetic counselor for result disclosure and management

actions.title: Penetrance and expressivity assessment
actions.description: Evaluate age-related penetrance, family history, and modifying factors for the specific variant-gene-disease triad

actions.title: Disease-specific surveillance protocol
actions.description: Implement evidence-based screening and risk-reduction strategies per professional society guidelines

actions.title: Cascade testing of at-risk relatives
actions.description: Offer targeted testing to first-degree relatives; discuss implications for second-degree relatives

actions.title: Insurance and psychosocial counseling
actions.description: Discuss GINA protections (US), insurance implications, and psychological impact of genetic testing results

connective_tissue.mechanism: Structural protein genes (collagens, fibrillin, TGF-beta pathway) providing tissue integrity and elasticity

outcomes.description: Favorable connective tissue integrity — low risk of adverse outcomes
Moderate connective tissue integrity — some genetic predisposition
Elevated connective tissue integrity — increased genetic risk requiring monitoring

actions.title: Discuss connective tissue integrity with healthcare provider
actions.description: Review findings and determine appropriate screening or monitoring

actions.title: Evidence-based tissue health optimization
actions.description: Implement recommended lifestyle and preventive measures

cyp2c19_metabolism.mechanism: CYP2C19 enzyme activity determined primarily by CYP2C19*2 (c.681G>A, rs4244285), CYP2C19*3 (c.636G>A, rs4986893), and CYP2C19*17 (c.-806C>T, rs12248560) variants, producing poor, intermediate, normal, and ultrarapid metabolizer phenotypes affecting drug metabolism of clopidogrel, proton pump inhibitors, antidepressants, and voriconazole

outcomes.description: Reduced clopidogrel activation with increased stent thrombosis risk; increased PPI efficacy
Reduced PPI efficacy for GERD/H. pylori; standard clopidogrel efficacy
Intermediate enzyme activity with moderate reduction in prodrug activation
Altered escitalopram and sertraline metabolism affecting efficacy and tolerability

actions.title: CYP2C19 genotype/phenotype determination
actions.description: Test for *2, *3, and *17 alleles to classify metabolizer status per CPIC guidelines

actions.title: Alternative antiplatelet for CYP2C19 poor metabolizers
actions.description: Use prasugrel or ticagrelor instead of clopidogrel in CYP2C19 poor metabolizers undergoing PCI

actions.title: PPI dose adjustment for ultrarapid metabolizers
actions.description: Consider increasing PPI dose or using twice-daily dosing in CYP2C19*17/*17 for GERD management

actions.title: Antidepressant selection and dosing
actions.description: Avoid escitalopram and sertraline in ultrarapid metabolizers; consider citalopram dose reduction in poor metabolizers

cyp2c9_metabolism.mechanism: CYP2C9 enzyme activity affected by CYP2C9*2 (c.430C>T, rs1799853) and CYP2C9*3 (c.1075A>C, rs1057910) loss-of-function variants, reducing clearance of CYP2C9 substrates including warfarin (S-warfarin), phenytoin, glipizide, tolbutamide, losartan, and multiple NSAIDs

outcomes.description: Reduced clearance of CYP2C9 substrates requiring dose reduction of affected drugs
Increased sensitivity to warfarin with higher bleeding risk at standard doses
Elevated phenytoin levels with increased risk of nystagmus, ataxia, and CNS toxicity
Potentially increased NSAID exposure; relevance for GI and renal adverse effects

actions.title: CYP2C9 genotype-guided dosing
actions.description: Use genotype to guide warfarin dosing algorithm; reduce phenytoin loading and maintenance doses for *2/*3 carriers

actions.title: Consider DOAC over warfarin
actions.description: DOACs (apixaban, rivaroxaban, edoxaban) may be preferred to avoid CYP2C9-dependent warfarin metabolism

actions.title: Enhanced phenytoin therapeutic monitoring
actions.description: More frequent serum phenytoin level checks, especially during dose titration; consider fosphenytoin loading

actions.title: NSAID use caution
actions.description: Use lowest effective NSAID dose; consider alternative analgesics in CYP2C9 poor metabolizers

cyp2d6_metabolism.mechanism: CYP2D6 enzyme activity determined by highly polymorphic gene locus with over 100 star-allele variants, copy number variation (gene deletions and multiplications), and hybridization with CYP2D7 pseudogene, resulting in a wide spectrum from poor to ultrarapid metabolizer phenotypes affecting metabolism of ~25% of all prescribed drugs

outcomes.description: Reduced prodrug activation (codeine, tramadol) and elevated concentrations of substrate drugs (metoprolol, antidepressants)
Rapid drug clearance reducing efficacy; toxicity from prodrugs due to accelerated conversion to active metabolites
Intermediate enzyme activity with dose-dependent drug response variability

actions.title: CYP2D6 genotype/phenotype determination
actions.description: Determine activity score from star alleles to classify as poor, intermediate, normal, or ultrarapid metabolizer

actions.title: Dose adjustment of CYP2D6 substrates
actions.description: Reduce doses of CYP2D6-metabolized drugs in poor metabolizers; increase doses or choose alternatives in ultrarapid metabolizers

actions.title: Avoid prodrugs requiring CYP2D6 activation
actions.description: Poor metabolizers: avoid codeine, tramadol, tamoxifen; use alternatives not requiring CYP2D6 activation

actions.title: Drug interaction management
actions.description: Avoid potent CYP2D6 inhibitors (paroxetine, fluoxetine, bupropion) that can phenoconvert to poor metabolizer status

cyp3a_metabolism.mechanism: CYP3A4 and CYP3A5 enzyme activity influenced by genetic variants including CYP3A5*3 (c.6986A>G, rs776746) causing alternative splicing and loss of CYP3A5 expression, and CYP3A4*22 (c.-392G>A, rs35599367) reducing CYP3A4 expression, with CYP3A also being highly susceptible to drug-drug interactions via induction and inhibition

outcomes.description: Loss of CYP3A5 activity affecting tacrolimus dosing, statin metabolism, and other substrate drugs
Reduced CYP3A4 expression (CYP3A4*22 carriers) affecting tacrolimus, statin, and midazolam clearance
Significantly higher tacrolimus dose requirements in CYP3A5 expressers; lower doses in CYP3A4*22 carriers
High susceptibility to CYP3A-mediated drug-drug interactions affecting a wide range of medications

actions.title: CYP3A5-guided tacrolimus dosing
actions.description: CYP3A5 expressers require 1.5-2x standard tacrolimus starting dose; use FDA-approved dosing tables

actions.title: Tacrolimus therapeutic drug monitoring
actions.description: Frequent trough monitoring during tacrolimus initiation and after interacting drugs added/removed

actions.title: Consider CYP3A4 statin metabolism
actions.description: Atorvastatin and simvastatin are CYP3A4 substrates; pravastatin and rosuvastatin are not

actions.title: Comprehensive drug interaction assessment
actions.description: Review all medications for CYP3A4/5 interactions including strong inhibitors (clarithromycin, itraconazole, ritonavir) and inducers (rifampin, carbamazepine, St. John's wort)

outcomes.description: Lower ability to clear toxins

dna_repair.mechanism: PARP enzyme activity and DNA damage response

outcomes.description: Accelerated cellular aging
Modified dopamine levels
Modified reward pathway sensitivity

drug_hypersensitivity.mechanism: Specific HLA alleles that present drug-derived antigens to T-cells, triggering immune-mediated hypersensitivity reactions. Well-characterized associations include HLA-B*57:01 with abacavir, HLA-B*15:02 with carbamazepine, HLA-A*31:01 with carbamazepine, HLA-B*58:01 with allopurinol, and HLA-B*57:03 with flucloxacillin

outcomes.description: Abacavir hypersensitivity reaction (fever, rash, GI symptoms, respiratory distress) in HLA-B*57:01 carriers
Stevens-Johnson syndrome / toxic epidermal necrolysis from carbamazepine in HLA-B*15:02 carriers
Stevens-Johnson syndrome / DRESS from allopurinol in HLA-B*58:01 carriers
Mild to moderate cutaneous adverse drug reactions (maculopapular exanthem)

actions.title: Pre-treatment HLA screening
actions.description: Mandatory HLA-B*57:01 screening before abacavir; recommended for HLA-B*15:02 / HLA-A*31:01 before carbamazepine in at-risk populations

actions.title: Avoid culprit medication
actions.description: Contraindicated: abacavir in HLA-B*57:01+; carbamazepine in HLA-B*15:02+; allopurinol in HLA-B*58:01+

actions.title: Select alternative medication
actions.description: Choose alternative ARV (e.g., tenofovir/emtricitabine instead of abacavir), anticonvulsant, or urate-lowering therapy

actions.title: Patient education on hypersensitivity
actions.description: Educate on early recognition of hypersensitivity symptoms; importance of immediate medical evaluation

outcomes.description: Modified drug clearance rates
Modified drug absorption and distribution
Impaired blood vessel function
Modified energy homeostasis
May indicate accelerated biological aging

actions.title: Optional: TruDiagnostic or MyDNAge
actions.description: Clocks disagree — do not act on a single reading; use for longitudinal tracking only

epigenetic_maintenance.mechanism: Histone modifications, DNA methylation, chromatin remodeling — the epigenomic landscape of aging

outcomes.description: Progressive loss of epigenetic information correlates with biological age
Loss of heterochromatin and derepression of repetitive elements

actions.title: Exercise slows epigenetic aging
actions.description: Physically active individuals show younger epigenetic age by multiple clock measures

actions.title: Dietary HDAC inhibitors (sulforaphane, curcumin, butyrate)
actions.description: Modest epigenetic effects; sulforaphane and fiber-derived butyrate have strongest evidence

actions.title: Track epigenetic age every 1-2 years
actions.description: Use for longitudinal trend tracking; do not act on a single clock change

outcomes.description: Impaired energy production during exercise

eye_health.mechanism: Retinal and ocular development genes protecting photoreceptors and retinal pigment epithelium from degeneration

outcomes.description: Favorable eye health — low risk of adverse outcomes
Moderate eye health — some genetic predisposition
Elevated eye health — increased genetic risk requiring monitoring

actions.title: Discuss eye health with healthcare provider
actions.description: Review findings and determine appropriate screening or monitoring

actions.title: Evidence-based vision protection optimization
actions.description: Implement recommended lifestyle and preventive measures

outcomes.description: Modified fat oxidation capacity

fluoropyrimidine_toxicity.mechanism: DPYD genetic variants (primarily c.1905+1G>A, c.1679T>G, c.2846A>T, and c.1236G>A) causing partial or complete dihydropyrimidine dehydrogenase deficiency, leading to severe and potentially fatal toxicity from 5-fluorouracil, capecitabine, and tegafur

outcomes.description: Grade 3-4 toxicity including severe mucositis, diarrhea, neutropenia, and hand-foot syndrome
Treatment-related death from fluoropyrimidine chemotherapy in DPYD-deficient patients
Inability to complete intended chemotherapy course due to toxicity

actions.title: Pre-treatment DPYD genotyping
actions.description: Recommended DPYD testing before initiating any fluoropyrimidine chemotherapy (DPYD-IS update pending)

actions.title: DPYD-guided dose reduction
actions.description: Reduce fluoropyrimidine dose by 25-50% for intermediate metabolizers; avoid for poor metabolizers

actions.title: Alternative chemotherapy regimen
actions.description: Consider non-fluoropyrimidine-based regimens for complete DPD deficiency

actions.title: Enhanced toxicity monitoring
actions.description: Close clinical monitoring for early signs of mucositis, diarrhea, and myelosuppression

gene_therapy_aging.mechanism: AAV-delivered gene therapies targeting aging pathways (klotho, follistatin, TERT)

outcomes.description: AAV delivery to sufficient cell populations remains challenging
Pre-existing AAV immunity limits treatment window

actions.title: Monitor AAV gene therapy for aging
actions.description: Field is pre-clinical to early Phase I. No approved therapies for aging indications

actions.title: Avoid telomerase-as-a-pill products
actions.description: The 2022 PNAS MCMV-TERT paper (+41% lifespan) was retracted August 2025. No credible telomerase pill evidence

genome_stability.mechanism: DNA repair pathways (NER, BER, HR, NHEJ, MMR) and genome maintenance machinery

outcomes.description: Unrepaired DNA damage is a primary driver of aging and cancer
Mutations in WRN, BLM, ERCC genes cause accelerated aging phenotypes

actions.title: Minimize UV, smoking, and genotoxic exposures
actions.description: Environmental DNA damage overwhelms repair capacity over time

actions.title: Support NAD+ for PARP-mediated DNA repair
actions.description: PARP1 consumes NAD+ during DNA repair; NAD+ availability may become limiting with age

actions.title: Age-appropriate cancer screening
actions.description: Genome instability increases cancer risk; screening is the most effective preventive action

outcomes.description: Driver of metabolic syndrome

actions.title: Annual HbA1c, fasting glucose, fasting insulin
Consider 2-week CGM for comprehensive glucose data
actions.description: More signal than single fasting measurement

actions.title: Discuss GLP-1 (semaglutide) with physician if indicated
actions.description: Showing biological age effects beyond weight loss

outcomes.description: Altered glucose metabolism

health_profile.mechanism: Broad genetic profile across multiple body systems with no identified high-risk variants in well-characterized disease genes

outcomes.description: Favorable overall health profile — low risk of adverse outcomes
Moderate overall health profile — some genetic predisposition
Elevated overall health profile — increased genetic risk requiring monitoring

actions.title: Discuss overall health profile with healthcare provider
actions.description: Review findings and determine appropriate screening or monitoring

actions.title: Evidence-based overall health optimization
actions.description: Implement recommended lifestyle and preventive measures

hearing_health.mechanism: Inner ear hair cell and cochlear development genes maintaining auditory function

outcomes.description: Favorable hearing function — low risk of adverse outcomes
Moderate hearing function — some genetic predisposition
Elevated hearing function — increased genetic risk requiring monitoring

actions.title: Discuss hearing function with healthcare provider
actions.description: Review findings and determine appropriate screening or monitoring

actions.title: Evidence-based hearing optimization
actions.description: Implement recommended lifestyle and preventive measures

outcomes.description: Impaired heme breakdown

hereditary_cancer.mechanism: Tumor suppressor and DNA repair genes (BRCA1/2, TP53, mismatch repair) guarding against malignant transformation

outcomes.description: Favorable cancer protection — low risk of adverse outcomes
Moderate cancer protection — some genetic predisposition
Elevated cancer protection — increased genetic risk requiring monitoring

actions.description: Review findings and determine appropriate screening or monitoring

outcomes.description: Headaches, itching, flushing
Modified oxygen utilization

immune_rejuvenation.mechanism: Reversal of immunosenescence — T-cell exhaustion, thymic involution, inflammaging

outcomes.description: Age-related decline in immune function increases infection and cancer risk
Chronic low-grade inflammation from aged immune cells

actions.title: Urolithin A for naive T-cell expansion
actions.description: Expands naive-like CD8+ T cells (preclinical, single study 2025)

actions.title: Monitor TPE+IVIG clinical trial results
actions.description: TPE+IVIG RCT showed immune-cell composition shift and biological age reduction (2025)

actions.title: Monitor thymic regeneration research
actions.description: TRIIM protocol (GH + DHEA + metformin) showed thymic regeneration in small trial; needs replication

immune_response.mechanism: Immune system regulatory genes (HLA, cytokine receptors, transcription factors) influencing predisposition to autoimmune conditions and infection response

outcomes.description: Favorable immune regulation — low risk of adverse outcomes
Moderate immune regulation — some genetic predisposition
Elevated immune regulation — increased genetic risk requiring monitoring

actions.title: Discuss immune regulation with healthcare provider
actions.description: Review findings and determine appropriate screening or monitoring

actions.title: Evidence-based immune health optimization
actions.description: Implement recommended lifestyle and preventive measures

outcomes.description: Accelerated aging, disease risk
Increased baseline inflammation
Increased Type 2 diabetes risk
Difficulty managing blood sugar

intermittent_fasting.mechanism: Periodic fasting activates autophagy and reduces insulin/IGF-1 signaling

outcomes.description: Cellular cleanup activated during fasting periods
Improved glucose regulation

actions.title: Consider 16:8 or 5:2 fasting protocol
actions.description: High-compliance fasters show age-opposing molecular trajectories within 6 months (TirolGesund 2025)

actions.title: Consult physician before extended fasts
actions.description: Not recommended for underweight, pregnant, or those with eating disorder history

irinotecan_toxicity.mechanism: UGT1A1 genetic variants (particularly UGT1A1*28 TA repeat polymorphism) reducing UDP-glucuronosyltransferase 1A1 enzyme activity, impairing glucuronidation of SN-38 (the active metabolite of irinotecan) and leading to severe neutropenia and diarrhea

outcomes.description: Grade 3-4 neutropenia with increased infection risk at standard irinotecan doses
Severe delayed-onset diarrhea requiring intensive supportive care and dose reduction
Inability to tolerate therapeutic irinotecan doses requiring regimen modification

actions.title: Pre-treatment UGT1A1 genotyping
actions.description: UGT1A1*28 genotyping recommended for irinotecan doses >= 150 mg/m2 (FDA label)

actions.title: UGT1A1-guided dose reduction
actions.description: Reduce starting irinotecan dose by 25-30% for UGT1A1 7/7 (poor metabolizer) genotypes

actions.title: Aggressive supportive care
actions.description: Intensive monitoring and early intervention for neutropenia and diarrhea

actions.title: Consider alternative chemotherapy
actions.description: Consider non-irinotecan-containing regimens for patients with severe UGT1A1 deficiency

outcomes.description: Progressive iron buildup
Iron accumulation in organs

kidney_health.mechanism: Renal structural and transport genes (collagen IV, polycystins, nephrocystins) maintaining kidney filtration

outcomes.description: Favorable kidney function — low risk of adverse outcomes
Moderate kidney function — some genetic predisposition
Elevated kidney function — increased genetic risk requiring monitoring

actions.title: Discuss kidney function with healthcare provider
actions.description: Review findings and determine appropriate screening or monitoring

actions.title: Evidence-based kidney health optimization
actions.description: Implement recommended lifestyle and preventive measures

klotho_anti_aging.mechanism: Klotho protein — systemic anti-aging factor; membrane and soluble forms regulate FGF23, Wnt, and ion homeostasis

outcomes.description: Klotho levels decline with age; low klotho associated with cognitive decline, kidney disease, cardiovascular disease
Higher klotho levels protect against cognitive decline — KL-VS haplotype carriers show enhanced cognition

actions.title: Aerobic exercise increases klotho levels
actions.description: Multiple studies show exercise acutely and chronically increases circulating klotho

actions.title: Monitor klotho gene therapy pipeline
actions.description: AAV-delivered s-KL extended mouse lifespan; Klotho Neurosciences has IND-track pipeline

actions.title: Maintain adequate vitamin D (regulates klotho expression)
actions.description: Vitamin D response elements present in klotho promoter

klotho_gene_therapy.mechanism: Klotho protein acts as systemic anti-aging factor; declines with age

outcomes.description: Age-related klotho decline linked to cognitive and kidney function loss
Higher klotho levels associated with cognitive resilience

actions.title: Monitor klotho gene therapy pipeline
actions.description: Klotho Neurosciences s-KL pipeline; AAV-delivered s-KL extended mouse lifespan; watch for IND filing

outcomes.description: Bloating, gas, digestive issues
Progeria-related aging pathway
Reduced LDL clearance

lipid_composition.mechanism: ELOVL2-mediated very long chain fatty acid synthesis

outcomes.description: Slightly modified cell membrane composition

lipid_disorder.mechanism: Monogenic and polygenic lipid disorders caused by variants in LDLR, APOB, PCSK9 (familial hypercholesterolemia), LPL, APOC2 (hypertriglyceridemia), ABCA1, LCAT (low HDL), and LIPA (lysosomal acid lipase deficiency), leading to severely abnormal lipid profiles

outcomes.description: Severely elevated LDL cholesterol from LDLR, APOB, or PCSK9 pathogenic variants
Markedly elevated triglycerides with pancreatitis risk from LPL or APOC2 deficiency
Premature atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease before age 55 (men) or 65 (women)
Achilles tendon and extensor tendon xanthomas in familial hypercholesterolemia

actions.title: Referral to lipid specialist
actions.description: Comprehensive lipid panel, Lp(a), apoB, and specialist evaluation for monogenic lipid disorder

actions.title: High-intensity statin therapy
actions.description: High-intensity statin (atorvastatin 40-80mg or rosuvastatin 20-40mg) as first-line therapy

actions.title: Combination lipid-lowering therapy
actions.description: Add ezetimibe, PCSK9 inhibitor, or bempedoic acid if LDL targets not met on statin alone

actions.title: Consider lipoprotein apheresis
actions.description: LDL apheresis for homozygous FH or severe heterozygous FH refractory to medications

actions.title: Cascade family screening
actions.description: First-degree relative lipid testing and genetic testing for identified familial variant

lipid_management.mechanism: Apolipoprotein B as primary lipid risk factor

outcomes.description: Direct measure of atherogenic particles

actions.title: Request apoB test (more predictive than LDL-C)
actions.description: Target: <80 mg/dL primary prevention, <60 high-risk

actions.title: Statin therapy if indicated
actions.description: Generic atorvastatin or rosuvastatin first-line

lipid_metabolism.mechanism: APOB and PCSK9 regulation of LDL cholesterol

outcomes.description: Increased cardiovascular risk
Increased liver fat storage
Altered lipid metabolism and cardiovascular risk
Potential cognitive health implications

liver_health.mechanism: Hepatic metabolism and detoxification genes maintaining liver function and regenerative capacity

outcomes.description: Favorable liver function — low risk of adverse outcomes
Moderate liver function — some genetic predisposition
Elevated liver function — increased genetic risk requiring monitoring

actions.title: Discuss liver function with healthcare provider
actions.description: Review findings and determine appropriate screening or monitoring

actions.title: Evidence-based liver health optimization
actions.description: Implement recommended lifestyle and preventive measures

outcomes.description: Associated with exceptional longevity
Modified cellular aging pathways

mTOR_signaling.mechanism: mTORC1/mTORC2 — master nutrient and growth signaling hub; integrates insulin, amino acids, energy status

outcomes.description: Chronic mTORC1 overactivation drives aging through increased translation, reduced autophagy, and metabolic dysfunction
mTORC2 dysregulation contributes to insulin resistance with age

actions.title: Discuss rapamycin (mTORC1 inhibitor) with longevity-literate physician
actions.description: Pulse dosing 5-10mg weekly; PEARL RCT showed healthspan marker improvement at 1 year

actions.title: Moderate protein restriction or cycling
actions.description: Lower protein intake reduces mTORC1 signaling; balance against sarcopenia risk in older adults

actions.title: Exercise provides tissue-specific mTOR benefits
actions.description: Resistance exercise activates mTORC1 in muscle for hypertrophy while systemic effects differ

malignant_hyperthermia.mechanism: Pathogenic variants in RYR1 (ryanodine receptor) and CACNA1S (calcium channel) genes causing abnormal calcium release from skeletal muscle sarcoplasmic reticulum in response to volatile anesthetics (halothane, sevoflurane, desflurane) and succinylcholine, leading to a life-threatening hypermetabolic crisis

outcomes.description: Life-threatening hyperthermia, muscle rigidity, rhabdomyolysis, acidosis, and cardiac arrest during anesthesia
Confirmed MH susceptibility requiring avoidance of triggering agents for life
Increased risk of exertional rhabdomyolysis and heat stroke in RYR1 variant carriers

actions.title: Medical alert identification
actions.description: Wear medical alert bracelet and carry MH susceptibility card at all times

actions.title: MH-safe anesthesia protocol
actions.description: Inform anesthesiologist: use only non-triggering agents (propofol, ketamine, opioids, nondepolarizing NMBs)

actions.title: Dantrolene and MH cart availability
actions.description: Ensure surgical facility has dantrolene and MH protocol; elective surgery at MH-ready centers

actions.title: Family cascade testing for MH
actions.description: All first-degree relatives should undergo genetic testing for the identified RYR1/CACNA1S variant

actions.title: Heat stroke precautions
actions.description: Avoid extreme heat, intense exercise in hot environments, and maintain hydration

outcomes.description: Increased cardiovascular risk
Fatigue and cognitive effects

actions.title: Take methylfolate
actions.description: 400-800mcg methylfolate daily

outcomes.description: Lower mitochondrial density
Reduced mitochondrial efficiency

monogenic_diabetes.mechanism: Monogenic diabetes resulting from variants in beta-cell genes including GCK (MODY2), HNF1A (MODY3), HNF4A (MODY1), HNF1B (MODY5), and KCNJ11/ABCC8 (neonatal diabetes), causing impaired insulin secretion rather than insulin resistance

outcomes.description: GCK-MODY: mild, stable fasting hyperglycemia from birth; rarely requires treatment beyond diet
HNF1A-MODY: progressive hyperglycemia typically diagnosed in adolescence/young adulthood
HNF1B-MODY with renal cysts and genitourinary abnormalities
Neonatal diabetes mellitus from KCNJ11/ABCC8 variants, treatable with sulfonylureas

actions.title: Reclassify diabetes subtype
actions.description: Distinguish monogenic diabetes from type 1 and type 2 diabetes; C-peptide and autoantibody testing

actions.title: Sulfonylurea transition
actions.description: Transition HNF1A/HNF4A-MODY and KCNJ11/ABCC8 neonatal diabetes from insulin to sulfonylurea therapy

actions.title: GCK-MODY: no pharmacotherapy
actions.description: GCK-MODY rarely requires pharmacotherapy; avoid unnecessary insulin or oral hypoglycemics

actions.title: Cascade family screening for MODY
actions.description: Test first-degree relatives for the identified MODY variant; up to 50% may be affected

outcomes.description: Natural predisposition to certain exercise types

muscular_strength.mechanism: Skeletal muscle mass and strength capacity

outcomes.description: Age-related muscle loss

actions.title: 2x/week full-body compound training
actions.description: Squat, hinge, press, pull, carry - 30-45 min

actions.title: Annual hand dynamometer test ($30)
actions.description: Simple proxy for overall strength

outcomes.description: Reduced NAD+ levels affecting sirtuins

neurodegeneration_risk.mechanism: Genetic variants conferring increased susceptibility to neurodegenerative diseases including Alzheimer disease (APOE e4, PSEN1/2, APP), Parkinson disease (LRRK2, GBA, SNCA, PRKN), and ALS (C9orf72, SOD1, TARDBP, FUS)

outcomes.description: Elevated lifetime risk of Alzheimer disease, particularly APOE e4 carriers
Elevated risk of Parkinson disease from LRRK2, GBA, and other variants
Elevated risk of amyotrophic lateral sclerosis from C9orf72, SOD1 variants
Early-onset disease in high-penetrance variants (PSEN1, APP, SOD1)

actions.title: Referral to neurology specialist
actions.description: Comprehensive neurological evaluation and baseline cognitive assessment

actions.title: Serial cognitive monitoring
actions.description: Regular cognitive screening (MoCA, Montreal Cognitive Assessment) for early detection

actions.title: Neuroprotective lifestyle interventions
actions.description: Cognitive stimulation, social engagement, Mediterranean diet, physical exercise

actions.title: Cardiovascular risk factor control
actions.description: Aggressive management of hypertension, diabetes, and hyperlipidemia for brain health

actions.title: Clinical trial consideration
actions.description: Discuss eligibility for preventive trials and disease-modifying therapies

actions.title: Genetic counseling for family planning
actions.description: Discuss implications for family members and reproductive options for high-penetrance variants

neurological_health.mechanism: Neuronal function and maintenance genes protecting against neurodegenerative processes

outcomes.description: Favorable neurological function — low risk of adverse outcomes
Moderate neurological function — some genetic predisposition
Elevated neurological function — increased genetic risk requiring monitoring

actions.title: Discuss neurological function with healthcare provider
actions.description: Review findings and determine appropriate screening or monitoring

actions.title: Evidence-based brain health optimization
actions.description: Implement recommended lifestyle and preventive measures

outcomes.description: Diminished brain adaptability
Modified neurotransmitter levels
Impaired conversion to long-chain omega-3s
Slightly reduced oxidative metabolism
Accelerated aging at cellular level
Diminished cellular protection

partial_reprogramming.mechanism: Transient expression of Yamanaka factors (OSK/OSKM) to reverse mesenchymal drift aging signature

outcomes.description: Age-related shift toward mesenchymal cell states — primary target of partial reprogramming
Full reprogramming risk: teratoma formation. Partial avoids this but delivery methods still being optimized

actions.title: Monitor clinical trials (NewLimit, Retro Biosciences)
actions.description: AAV-OSK extended lifespan 109% in old mice; first-in-human trials pending

actions.title: Track epigenetic age longitudinally
actions.description: Use clocks to track mesenchymal drift reversal if reprogramming therapies become available

proteasome_autophagy.mechanism: Macroautophagy, chaperone-mediated autophagy, and ubiquitin-proteasome system clearance

outcomes.description: Age-related decline in autophagy impairs clearance of damaged proteins and organelles
Protein aggregates (tau, amyloid, lipofuscin) accumulate with declining clearance

actions.title: Intermittent fasting to induce autophagy
actions.description: Fasting periods >16h trigger significant autophagy upregulation

actions.title: Exercise to stimulate autophagy
actions.description: Both aerobic and resistance exercise activate autophagy in muscle and brain

actions.title: Spermidine-rich foods (wheat germ, aged cheese, mushrooms)
actions.description: Polyamine that induces autophagy; epidemiological associations with lower mortality

protein_homeostasis.mechanism: Chaperone networks (HSP70/HSP90/HSP60) and ubiquitin-proteasome quality control

outcomes.description: Accumulation of misfolded proteins drives neurodegeneration and aging
Age-related decline in heat shock response impairs cellular stress resistance

actions.title: Sauna / heat exposure (4-7x/week)
actions.description: Finnish sauna cohort: 4-7x/week associated with 40% lower all-cause mortality, 63% lower cardiovascular mortality

actions.title: Exercise to induce HSP response
actions.description: Exercise transiently upregulates heat shock proteins and proteasome activity

actions.title: Sulforaphane (broccoli sprouts) for NRF2 activation
actions.description: Activates NRF2 pathway which regulates proteasome subunits and chaperones

outcomes.description: Requires physician supervision

actions.title: If prescribed: 5-10mg weekly (pulse, not daily)
actions.description: PEARL RCT showed improved healthspan markers at 1 year

rare_functional_variant.mechanism: Rare genetic variant (allele frequency < 1%) predicted to cause a functional effect on protein coding, splicing, or regulatory elements, but of uncertain or uncharacterized clinical significance requiring systematic evaluation of population frequency, in silico predictions, literature evidence, and segregation data

outcomes.description: Variant of uncertain significance (VUS) requiring ongoing reclassification efforts
Variant with conflicting or limited evidence for pathogenicity; may be reclassified
Incidental or secondary finding unrelated to primary testing indication
Carrier status for a recessive condition without clinical phenotype

actions.title: Systematic variant classification
actions.description: Apply ACMG/AMP criteria for variant classification; check ClinVar, gnomAD, and literature databases

actions.title: Periodic literature reevaluation
actions.description: Reclassify variant every 2-5 years as new evidence becomes available; many VUS are reclassified

actions.title: Family segregation analysis
actions.description: Test affected and unaffected family members to assess variant cosegregation with phenotype

actions.title: Functional characterization studies
actions.description: In vitro or in vivo functional studies to assess variant effect on protein function

actions.title: Expert consultation and multidisciplinary review
actions.description: Consultation with clinical genetics, molecular pathology, and disease-specific specialists for complex cases

respiratory_health.mechanism: Lung development and surfactant genes maintaining airway function and gas exchange

outcomes.description: Favorable respiratory function — low risk of adverse outcomes
Moderate respiratory function — some genetic predisposition
Elevated respiratory function — increased genetic risk requiring monitoring

actions.title: Discuss respiratory function with healthcare provider
actions.description: Review findings and determine appropriate screening or monitoring

actions.title: Evidence-based lung health optimization
actions.description: Implement recommended lifestyle and preventive measures

senescence.mechanism: Cellular senescence — irreversible cell cycle arrest with pro-inflammatory SASP secretion

outcomes.description: Senescence-associated secretory phenotype drives chronic inflammation
Senescent cell accumulation impairs tissue repair and regeneration

actions.title: Discuss D+Q senolytic protocol with physician
actions.description: Dasatinib + quercetin once-monthly; most evidence for senescent cell clearance

actions.title: Regular exercise reduces senescent cell burden
actions.description: Exercise lowers p16INK4a expression and senescent cell markers

actions.title: Dietary fisetin (strawberries, apples, persimmons)
actions.description: Natural senolytic; food sources provide lower doses than supplements

senolytic_protocol.mechanism: Clearing senescent cells that drive aging

outcomes.description: Senescent cells drive inflammatory aging

actions.title: D+Q (dasatinib + quercetin) once monthly
actions.description: Most evidence for senolytic effect

outcomes.description: Altered serotonin reuptake

skin_health.mechanism: Epidermal barrier and wound healing genes maintaining skin integrity and repair capacity

outcomes.description: Favorable skin integrity — low risk of adverse outcomes
Moderate skin integrity — some genetic predisposition
Elevated skin integrity — increased genetic risk requiring monitoring

actions.title: Discuss skin integrity with healthcare provider
actions.description: Review findings and determine appropriate screening or monitoring

actions.title: Evidence-based skin health optimization
actions.description: Implement recommended lifestyle and preventive measures

sleep_longevity.mechanism: Sleep duration and quality impact on cellular repair

outcomes.description: Accumulates disease risk

actions.title: Sleep hygiene: cool room, dark, no screens before bed

outcomes.description: Difficulty falling or staying asleep
Reduced next-day performance
Quitting at 25-34 recovers ~10 years of life

actions.title: Quit smoking
actions.description: Strongest lifestyle intervention

statin_myopathy.mechanism: SLCO1B1 variants (particularly c.521T>C, rs4149056) encoding reduced-function OATP1B1 transporter, impairing hepatic uptake of statins (particularly simvastatin, but also atorvastatin and pitavastatin), leading to elevated systemic statin exposure and increased risk of dose-dependent statin-induced myopathy

outcomes.description: Muscle pain without CK elevation, often leading to statin non-adherence
Muscle pain/symptoms with elevated creatine kinase levels
Severe muscle breakdown with CK > 10x ULN, risk of renal failure
Inability to tolerate any statin due to muscle symptoms

actions.title: SLCO1B1 genotype consideration
actions.description: Pre-treatment SLCO1B1 genotyping when considering simvastatin therapy (CPIC Level A guideline)

actions.title: Avoid high-dose simvastatin
actions.description: Avoid simvastatin > 20 mg in SLCO1B1 c.521TC; avoid simvastatin entirely in c.521CC homozygotes

actions.title: Use alternative statin
actions.description: Rosuvastatin or pravastatin are less dependent on OATP1B1 and have lower myopathy risk

actions.title: Lower dose with add-on therapy
actions.description: Low-dose rosuvastatin (5-10 mg) plus ezetimibe for patients unable to tolerate standard statin doses

actions.title: CoQ10 supplementation trial
actions.description: Consider Coenzyme Q10 supplementation; limited but potential benefit for statin myalgia

stem_cells.mechanism: Stem cell exhaustion is a hallmark of aging; replenishment may restore tissue function

outcomes.description: Declining stem cell pools impair tissue repair and regeneration
Unregulated stem cell clinics cause serious adverse events including infections and tumors

actions.title: Only consider stem cell therapies in registered clinical trials
actions.description: Avoid unregulated clinics. iPSC-derived therapies (e.g., brain immune rejuvenation) are preclinical only

actions.title: Exercise to support endogenous stem cell function
actions.description: Exercise mobilizes endogenous stem cells and supports niche function

supplements_evidence.mechanism: Evidence-based evaluation of common longevity supplements

outcomes.description: Most supplement-aisle longevity products lack RCT endpoint evidence

actions.title: Avoid NMN/NR monotherapy
actions.description: Blood NAD+ goes up; clinical endpoints do not (confirmed by latest large RCT, NR-long COVID-2025)

actions.title: Skip resveratrol and pterostilbene
actions.description: No RCT support at endpoint level for anti-aging claims

actions.title: Test vitamin D before supplementing
actions.description: VITAL RCT (n=25,871, 5.3yr): no reduction in cancer (HR 0.96), CVD (HR 0.97), or all-cause mortality (HR 0.99) in non-deficient adults

outcomes.description: Accelerated cellular aging
Modified metabolic heat production

thiopurine_toxicity.mechanism: TPMT and NUDT15 genetic variants leading to reduced thiopurine S-methyltransferase activity, causing accumulation of 6-thioguanine nucleotides and severe myelosuppression with standard doses of azathioprine, 6-mercaptopurine, and thioguanine

outcomes.description: Life-threatening bone marrow suppression with pancytopenia at standard thiopurine doses
Inability to tolerate therapeutic doses of thiopurine medications
Forced interruption of thiopurine therapy for IBD, autoimmune conditions, or leukemia

actions.title: Pre-treatment TPMT/NUDT15 testing
actions.description: Mandatory pharmacogenetic testing before initiating thiopurine therapy

actions.title: Empiric dose reduction
actions.description: Reduce starting dose by 30-80% based on TPMT/NUDT15 phenotype; homozygous deficient: avoid or use 10% dose

actions.title: Alternative immunosuppressant therapy
actions.description: Consider methotrexate, mycophenolate, or biologic therapy for TPMT-deficient patients

actions.title: Intensive CBC monitoring
actions.description: Weekly CBC monitoring during thiopurine dose titration for intermediate metabolizers

outcomes.description: Increased thrombosis risk

thyroid_function.mechanism: Thyroid hormone synthesis, transport, and receptor genes regulating metabolic rate and energy balance

outcomes.description: Favorable thyroid function — low risk of adverse outcomes
Moderate thyroid function — some genetic predisposition
Elevated thyroid function — increased genetic risk requiring monitoring

actions.title: Discuss thyroid function with healthcare provider
actions.description: Review findings and determine appropriate screening or monitoring

actions.title: Evidence-based metabolic health optimization
actions.description: Implement recommended lifestyle and preventive measures

trametinib_rapamycin.mechanism: Combined MEK + mTOR inhibition for synergistic longevity effects

outcomes.description: Combo outperforms rapamycin alone in ITP mouse studies
Trametinib has serious toxicity profile — melanoma drug, not wellness compound

actions.title: Wait for Phase II combination trial
actions.description: Both drugs FDA-approved separately; trametinib toxicity requires clinical-supervised combination studies before any off-label consideration

actions.title: Discuss rapamycin alone with longevity-literate physician first
actions.description: Rapamycin monotherapy has more safety data than combination approaches

outcomes.description: Increased cardiovascular risk
Increased gout and kidney stone risk
Age-related decline in mitophagy

actions.title: Consider Mitopure (urolithin A)
actions.description: 4-week RCT on immune endpoints in Nature Aging

outcomes.description: Reduced vitamin A from plant sources

actions.title: Include preformed vitamin A in diet
actions.description: Eat liver, eggs, dairy

outcomes.description: Bone density issues, immune dysfunction

actions.title: Take Vitamin D3
actions.description: 2000-4000 IU daily with K2

warfarin_sensitivity.mechanism: VKORC1 promoter variants (particularly -1639G>A) reducing vitamin K epoxide reductase complex expression, combined with CYP2C9 loss-of-function variants (*2, *3) reducing S-warfarin clearance, leading to heightened warfarin sensitivity and increased bleeding risk at standard doses

outcomes.description: Elevated risk of major bleeding including intracranial hemorrhage on standard warfarin doses
Extended time to reach stable therapeutic INR during warfarin initiation
Requirement for substantially lower warfarin doses (1-4 mg/day vs. typical 4-8 mg/day)

actions.title: Pharmacogenomic-guided warfarin dosing
actions.description: Use CPIC-endorsed algorithm incorporating VKORC1, CYP2C9 genotype, age, weight, and INR target

actions.title: Reduced loading and maintenance dose
actions.description: Typically 2-4 mg starting dose; avoid standard 5-10 mg loading in VKORC1 AA / CYP2C9 *2/*3 carriers

actions.title: Frequent INR monitoring during initiation
actions.description: More frequent INR checks during first 2-4 weeks of therapy to prevent supratherapeutic levels

actions.title: Consider DOAC alternative
actions.description: Direct oral anticoagulants (apixaban, rivaroxaban) may be safer alternatives to warfarin

developmental.mechanism: Embryonic development and morphogenesis pathways affecting structural formation

outcomes.description: Variation in genes affecting developmental processes may influence structural and functional traits

actions.title: Discuss developmental genetic findings with healthcare provider
actions.description: Review findings in context of personal and family history

actions.title: Monitor relevant structural or functional parameters
actions.description: Follow condition-specific screening guidelines if applicable

choline_metabolism.mechanism: PEMT-mediated endogenous choline synthesis from phosphatidylethanolamine; choline is essential for methylation (via betaine), membrane integrity, lipoprotein secretion, and neurotransmitter synthesis

outcomes.description: Reduced endogenous choline synthesis increases risk of fatty liver, muscle damage, and impaired methylation when dietary intake is insufficient
Choline is a major methyl donor — deficiency shifts methylation burden to folate pathway

actions.title: Include choline-rich foods: eggs (yolks), beef liver, soy lecithin, wheat germ, cruciferous vegetables
actions.description: Dietary choline compensates for reduced endogenous synthesis

actions.title: Consider citicoline or alpha-GPC supplementation (250-500mg/day)
actions.description: Supplemental choline forms that cross the blood-brain barrier effectively

actions.title: Monitor liver function (ALT, AST) and consider ultrasound if fatty liver is suspected
actions.description: Choline deficiency is a direct cause of non-alcoholic fatty liver disease

iron_homeostasis.mechanism: Hepcidin-ferroportin axis regulating dietary iron absorption and reticuloendothelial iron recycling; TMPRSS6 matriptase-2 modulates hepcidin expression

outcomes.description: Higher hepcidin reduces iron absorption, increasing risk of iron deficiency especially with plant-based diets or blood loss
Lower hepcidin increases iron absorption; combined with HFE hemochromatosis variants can lead to iron overload

actions.title: Monitor ferritin, serum iron, TIBC, and transferrin saturation annually
actions.description: Regular iron panel monitoring catches both deficiency and overload early

actions.title: Pair iron-rich foods with vitamin C to enhance absorption (if deficient)
actions.description: Vitamin C increases non-heme iron absorption 2-3x

actions.title: Avoid iron supplementation without documented deficiency and physician guidance
actions.description: Excess iron is pro-oxidative and contributes to aging via Fenton reaction

magnesium_status.mechanism: TRPM6-mediated intestinal magnesium absorption and renal reabsorption; magnesium is cofactor for 300+ enzymes including ATP metabolism, DNA repair, and neurotransmitter regulation

outcomes.description: Suboptimal magnesium status can impair energy production, sleep quality, muscle function, and cardiovascular health

actions.title: Supplement magnesium glycinate or malate (200-400mg elemental/day)
actions.description: Well-absorbed magnesium forms that don't cause GI distress

actions.title: Include leafy greens, nuts, seeds, dark chocolate, and legumes daily
actions.description: Dietary magnesium supports consistent daily intake

actions.title: Epsom salt baths or topical magnesium oil for transdermal absorption
actions.description: Alternative route that bypasses gut absorption issues

thyroid_metabolism.mechanism: Deiodinase enzymes (DIO1, DIO2) convert T4 to active T3 in peripheral tissues; selenium-dependent process crucial for metabolic rate, thermogenesis, and cognitive function

outcomes.description: Reduced peripheral T4→T3 conversion can cause tissue-level hypothyroidism despite normal TSH
Lower cellular T3 reduces basal metabolic rate and thermogenesis

actions.title: Ensure adequate selenium (100-200mcg/day) and zinc — required deiodinase cofactors
actions.description: Selenium and zinc are essential for deiodinase enzyme function

actions.title: Monitor TSH, free T3, free T4, and reverse T3 — not just TSH alone
actions.description: Comprehensive thyroid panel reveals conversion efficiency

actions.title: Maintain adequate but not excessive iodine (150-300mcg/day)
actions.description: Both deficiency and excess can impair thyroid function

selenium_metabolism.mechanism: Selenium incorporation into selenoproteins (GPX1, GPX4, SEPP1, DIO1/2/3) — critical for antioxidant defense, thyroid hormone metabolism, and ferroptosis protection

outcomes.description: Lower GPX activity increases vulnerability to oxidative stress and lipid peroxidation
GPX4 requires selenium to prevent ferroptotic cell death

actions.title: Selenium supplementation (selenomethionine 100-200mcg/day)
actions.description: Supports GPX and deiodinase enzyme activity

actions.title: 1-2 Brazil nuts/day provides full RDA of selenium
actions.description: Brazil nuts are the richest natural selenium source; don't exceed 3-4/day to avoid toxicity

zinc_homeostasis.mechanism: SLC39A8 (ZIP8) and SLC30A8 (ZnT8) transporters regulating cellular zinc uptake and distribution; zinc is essential for immune function, DNA repair, growth, and 300+ zinc-finger transcription factors

outcomes.description: Reduced cellular zinc can impair immune response, wound healing, and antioxidant defense

actions.title: Ensure adequate zinc intake (15-25mg/day; zinc picolinate or citrate)
actions.description: Well-absorbed zinc forms support immune and antioxidant function

actions.title: Include oysters, beef, pumpkin seeds, and lentils regularly
actions.description: Dietary zinc from animal sources is more bioavailable than plant sources

carrier_status.mechanism: Heterozygous pathogenic variant in a gene associated with an autosomal recessive condition. Carrier status typically does not cause disease but may be relevant for reproductive planning and family screening.

outcomes.description: Carrier of a recessive condition — generally healthy but may pass variant to offspring
If partner is also a carrier, 25% risk of affected child per pregnancy

actions.title: Discuss carrier status with a genetic counselor for family planning implications
actions.description: Carrier screening for partner and genetic counseling for reproductive options

actions.title: Inform family members — siblings have 50% chance of being carriers
actions.description: Cascade carrier testing for at-risk family members

actions.title: Understand the condition — carrier status is not a disease diagnosis
actions.description: Most carriers are healthy; knowledge empowers informed reproductive decisions

cardiovascular_polygenic.mechanism: Aggregate of common cardiovascular-associated variants (GWAS Catalog, genome-wide significant). Each variant is individually small-effect; the domain signal is their net direction.

outcomes.description: Population-level cardiovascular risk tendency across many common variants

actions.title: Monitor lipids and blood pressure on schedule
Discuss your polygenic profile with a clinician for context

metabolic_polygenic.mechanism: Aggregate of common metabolic-associated variants (glucose, insulin, body composition) at genome-wide significance.

outcomes.description: Population-level metabolic tendency across many common variants

actions.title: Discuss your polygenic profile with a clinician for context

longevity_polygenic.mechanism: Aggregate of common longevity- and healthspan-associated variants at genome-wide significance.

outcomes.description: Population-level healthspan tendency across many common variants

actions.title: Discuss your polygenic profile with a clinician for context

cognitive_polygenic.mechanism: Aggregate of common brain- and cognition-associated variants at genome-wide significance.

outcomes.description: Population-level cognitive-health tendency across many common variants

actions.title: Discuss your polygenic profile with a clinician for context

performance_polygenic.mechanism: Aggregate of common athletic-performance-associated variants (power vs endurance) at genome-wide significance.

outcomes.description: Population-level performance tendency across many common variants

actions.title: Match training modality to your profile
Discuss your polygenic profile with a clinician for context

sleep_polygenic.mechanism: Aggregate of common sleep- and circadian-associated variants at genome-wide significance.

outcomes.description: Population-level sleep/chronotype tendency across many common variants

actions.title: Discuss your polygenic profile with a clinician for context

immune_polygenic.mechanism: Aggregate of common immune- and inflammation-associated variants at genome-wide significance.

outcomes.description: Population-level immune/inflammatory tendency across many common variants

actions.title: Discuss your polygenic profile with a clinician for context

nutrition_polygenic.mechanism: Aggregate of common nutrition- and micronutrient-associated variants at genome-wide significance.

outcomes.description: Population-level nutrient-need tendency across many common variants

actions.title: Discuss your polygenic profile with a clinician for context

cancer_polygenic.mechanism: Aggregate of common cancer-risk-associated variants at genome-wide significance. This is a population-level tendency, not a hereditary cancer syndrome finding.

outcomes.description: Population-level cancer-risk tendency across many common variants; not diagnostic

actions.title: Discuss your polygenic profile with a clinician for context

other_traits_polygenic.mechanism: Aggregate of other common trait-associated variants documented in the GWAS Catalog at genome-wide significance.

outcomes.description: Population-level tendency across assorted common variants

actions.title: Discuss your polygenic profile with a clinician for context
